skill.name: ai-avatar-video
skill.description: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. Routes across ByteDance OmniHuman (audio-driven full-body avatar), Wan-AI Wan 2-7 (audio-driven mouth sync via `audio_url` on a portrait), HappyHorse 1.0 (Arena #1 t2v / i2v with in-pass audio), and Seedance v2 Pro (multi-modal cinematic with reference audio + reference subject). Picks the right model for the user's actual intent — UGC voiceover, virtual presenter, dubbed product demo, lip-synced character, dialog scene — and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke.
skill.summary: Create AI avatar, talking-head, and lip-sync videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 115 static findings across 303 lines. All flagged patterns are false positives. External command flags stem from markdown code syntax, not shell execution. Network flags are legitimate API documentation URLs. The skill has proper security controls: scoped allowed-tools (Bash(runcomfy *)), JSON-based input preventing shell injection, documented token storage with proper permissions, and deepfake ethics warnings.

medium_findings.title: Static Analysis Pattern False Positives
medium_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 67 external_command patterns and 5 weak_crypto patterns. All are false positives. Backtick patterns (SKILL.md lines 7-302) are markdown inline code syntax, not shell backtick execution. Weak crypto flags (lines 5, 76, 135, 200, 265) misidentified 'hash' in content hashes as cryptographic weakness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged backtick patterns appear in markdown code blocks and inline code as documentation examples. No actual shell command substitution or execution occurs.

low_findings.title: CLI Token Storage in User Config Directory
low_findings.description: The skill documents token storage at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. This is standard CLI practice with proper mode 0600 permissions. Not a vulnerability when properly scoped.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage in user config directory with restricted permissions is documented security practice, not a vulnerability.

content.user_title: Create AI Avatar and Talking Head Videos
content.value_statement: Generate professional avatar videos with lip-sync from audio files or scripts. Claude Code automates video creation through the RunComfy CLI with routes for different avatar styles and use cases.
actual_capabilities: Generate talking head videos from portrait image and audio file using OmniHuman
Create avatar videos from text scripts using HappyHorse 1.0 in-pass audio
Produce lip-synced videos with full scene control using Wan 2-7 audio_url
Animate stylized characters and mascots synchronized to audio with Wan 2-2 Animate
Compose cinematic avatar videos with multiple reference assets using Seedance v2 Pro
Route requests to appropriate model based on user intent and available inputs
limitations: Requires pre-recorded audio file or portrait for most routes
Audio quality directly affects lip-sync quality and video output
Generated content may be subject to platform-specific synthetic media policies

use_cases.title: Create UGC Product Ads
use_cases.description: Generate vertical video ads with a branded presenter avatar. Upload a portrait and voiceover MP3 to create consistent UGC content for social platforms.

use_cases.title: Produce Multi-Language Training Videos
use_cases.description: Create dubbed training content by reusing the same presenter portrait with different language audio files. Maintain consistent brand identity across localization.

use_cases.title: Generate AI Presenter Content
use_cases.description: Create corporate presentation videos from a script without recording. Use HappyHorse 1.0 to generate speech and video in a single pass.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Talking Head Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video. I have a portrait image and a voiceover audio file ready.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user has a specific portrait and audio file

prompt_templates.title: Script-Based Avatar Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking head video from this script. I do not have an audio file: [paste script here]
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides text but no audio recording

prompt_templates.title: Stylized Character Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an animated video of [character description] speaking from an audio file.
prompt_templates.scenario: For anime, illustration, or mascot characters

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Presenter Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic monologue video with [describe style, lighting, mood]. Reference subject from [image URL] and audio from [audio URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: For professional presentations with specific visual direction

output_examples.input: Create a talking head video from my portrait.jpg and voiceover.mp3
output_examples.output: Generated video using OmniHuman with 9:16 aspect ratio. The presenter in portrait.jpg now speaks the voiceover.mp3 with natural lip-sync. Output saved to ./out/

output_examples.input: Make a video where the character says 'Welcome to our demo' without an audio file
output_examples.output: Created video using HappyHorse 1.0 text-to-video. The generated character delivers the script with in-pass audio. 6-second clip at 1080p.

output_examples.input: Animate my mascot illustration with this voiceover track
output_examples.output: Used Wan 2-2 Animate to sync the mascot character to the audio. Full-body animation with character movement synchronized to speech.

best_practices: Use head-and-shoulders portrait framing for best talking head results
Provide clean voiceover audio without music beds for accurate lip-sync
Match audio emotional tone to face expression descriptions in prompts
anti_patterns: Do not use low-quality or noisy audio files that degrade lip-sync accuracy
Do not expect exact lip-sync with HappyHorse since audio is regenerated each call
Do not use full-body portraits expecting presenter-style talking head output

faq.question: What is the difference between OmniHuman and HappyHorse?
faq.answer: OmniHuman requires a pre-recorded audio file and portrait image. HappyHorse generates speech from a written script in-pass, no audio file needed.

faq.question: Can I use this for any portrait or character?
faq.answer: OmniHuman works best with photoreal portraits. For stylized characters, illustrations, or mascots, use Wan 2-2 Animate.

faq.question: How long can the generated videos be?
faq.answer: Duration depends on the model. OmniHuman and Wan 2-7 typically support 8-10 seconds per call. Chain multiple clips for longer content.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to sign in with `runcomfy login` or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable. The CLI manages API authentication.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Most models support common ratios like 9:16 (vertical), 16:9 (horizontal), and 1:1 (square). Check model documentation for full options.

faq.question: Are there content guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes. Ensure you have rights to both the subject likeness and audio voice. Follow deepfake disclosure norms and platform policies for synthetic media.
